[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache/controller.go", "content": "/*\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage cache\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n\tclientgofeaturegate \"k8s.io/client-go/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n)\n\n// This file implements a low-level controller that is used in\n// sharedIndexInformer, which is an implementation of\n// SharedIndexInformer.  Such informers, in turn, are key components\n// in the high level controllers that form the backbone of the\n// Kubernetes control plane.  Look at those for examples, or the\n// example in\n// https://github.com/kubernetes/client-go/tree/master/examples/workqueue\n// .\n\n// Config contains all the settings for one of these low-level controllers.\ntype Config struct {\n\t// The queue for your objects - has to be a DeltaFIFO due to\n\t// assumptions in the implementation. Your Process() function\n\t// should accept the output of this Queue's Pop() method.\n\tQueue\n\n\t// Something that can list and watch your objects.\n\tListerWatcher\n\n\t// Process can process a popped Deltas.\n\tProcess ProcessFunc\n\n\t// ProcessBatch can process a batch of popped Deltas, which should return `TransactionError` if not all items\n\t// in the batch were successfully processed.\n\t//\n\t// For batch processing to be used:\n\t// * ProcessBatch must be non-nil\n\t// * Queue must implement QueueWithBatch\n\t// * The client InOrderInformersBatchProcess feature gate must be enabled\n\t//\n\t// If any of those are false, Process is used and no batch processing is done.\n\tProcessBatch ProcessBatchFunc\n\n\t// ObjectType is an example object of the type this controller is\n\t// expected to handle.\n\tObjectType runtime.Object\n\n\t// ObjectDescription is the description to use when logging type-specific information about this controller.\n\tObjectDescription string\n\n\t// FullResyncPeriod is the period at which ShouldResync is considered.\n\tFullResyncPeriod time.Duration\n\n\t// MinWatchTimeout, if set, will define the minimum timeout for watch requests send\n\t// to kube-apiserver. However, values lower than 5m will not be honored to avoid\n\t// negative performance impact on controlplane.\n\t// Optional - if unset a default value of 5m will be used.\n\tMinWatchTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// ShouldResync is periodically used by the reflector to determine\n\t// whether to Resync the Queue. If ShouldResync is `nil` or\n\t// returns true, it means the reflector should proceed with the\n\t// resync.\n\tShouldResync ShouldResyncFunc\n\n\t// Called whenever the ListAndWatch drops the connection with an error.\n\t//\n\t// Contextual logging: WatchErrorHandlerWithContext should be used instead of WatchErrorHandler in code which supports contextual logging.\n\tWatchErrorHandler WatchErrorHandler\n\n\t// Called whenever the ListAndWatch drops the connection with an error\n\t// and WatchErrorHandler is not set.\n\tWatchErrorHandlerWithContext WatchErrorHandlerWithContext\n\n\t// WatchListPageSize is the requested chunk size of initial and relist watch lists.\n\tWatchListPageSize int64\n}\n\n// ShouldResyncFunc is a type of function that indicates if a reflector should perform a\n// resync or not. It can be used by a shared informer to support multiple event handlers with custom\n// resync periods.\ntype ShouldResyncFunc func() bool\n\n// ProcessFunc processes a single object.\ntype ProcessFunc func(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool) error\n\n// ProcessBatchFunc processes multiple objects in batch.\n// The deltas must not contain multiple entries for the same object.\ntype ProcessBatchFunc func(deltas []Delta, isInInitialList bool) error\n\n// `*controller` implements Controller\ntype controller struct {\n\tconfig         Config\n\treflector      *Reflector\n\treflectorMutex sync.RWMutex\n\tclock          clock.Clock\n}\n\n// Controller is a low-level controller that is parameterized by a\n// Config and used in sharedIndexInformer.\ntype Controller interface {\n\t// RunWithContext does two things.  One is to construct and run a Reflector\n\t// to pump objects/notifications from the Config's ListerWatcher\n\t// to the Config's Queue and possibly invoke the occasional Resync\n\t// on that Queue.  The other is to repeatedly Pop from the Queue\n\t// and process with the Config's ProcessFunc.  Both of these\n\t// continue until the context is canceled.\n\t//\n\t// It's an error to call RunWithContext more than once.\n\t// RunWithContext blocks; call via go.\n\tRunWithContext(ctx context.Context)\n\n\t// Run does the same as RunWithContext with a stop channel instead of\n\t// a context.\n\t//\n\t// Contextual logging: RunWithcontext should be used instead of Run in code which supports contextual logging.\n\tRun(stopCh <-chan struct{})\n\n\t// HasSynced delegates to the Config's Queue\n\tHasSynced() bool\n\n\t// LastSyncResourceVersion delegates to the Reflector when there\n\t// is one, otherwise returns the empty string\n\tLastSyncResourceVersion() string\n}\n\n// New makes a new Controller from the given Config.\nfunc New(c *Config) Controller {\n\tctlr := &controller{\n\t\tconfig: *c,\n\t\tclock:  &clock.RealClock{},\n\t}\n\treturn ctlr\n}\n\n// Run implements [Controller.Run].\nfunc (c *controller) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tc.RunWithContext(wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh))\n}\n\n// RunWithContext implements [Controller.RunWithContext].\nfunc (c *controller) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) {\n\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\t<-ctx.Done()\n\t\tc.config.Queue.Close()\n\t}()\n\tr := NewReflectorWithOptions(\n\t\tc.config.ListerWatcher,\n\t\tc.config.ObjectType,\n\t\tc.config.Queue,\n\t\tReflectorOptions{\n\t\t\tResyncPeriod:    c.config.FullResyncPeriod,\n\t\t\tMinWatchTimeout: c.config.MinWatchTimeout,\n\t\t\tTypeDescription: c.config.ObjectDescription,\n\t\t\tClock:           c.clock,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tr.ShouldResync = c.config.ShouldResync\n\tr.WatchListPageSize = c.config.WatchListPageSize\n\tif c.config.WatchErrorHandler != nil {\n\t\tr.watchErrorHandler = func(_ context.Context, r *Reflector, err error) {\n\t\t\tc.config.WatchErrorHandler(r, err)\n\t\t}\n\t} else if c.config.WatchErrorHandlerWithContext != nil {\n\t\tr.watchErrorHandler = c.config.WatchErrorHandlerWithContext\n\t}\n\n\tc.reflectorMutex.Lock()\n\tc.reflector = r\n\tc.reflectorMutex.Unlock()\n\n\tvar wg wait.Group\n\n\twg.StartWithContext(ctx, r.RunWithContext)\n\n\twait.UntilWithContext(ctx, c.processLoop, time.Second)\n\twg.Wait()\n}\n\n// Returns true once this controller has completed an initial resource listing\nfunc (c *controller) HasSynced() bool {\n\treturn c.config.Queue.HasSynced()\n}\n\nfunc (c *controller) LastSyncResourceVersion() string {\n\tc.reflectorMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer c.reflectorMutex.RUnlock()\n\tif c.reflector == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\treturn c.reflector.LastSyncResourceVersion()\n}\n\n// processLoop drains the work queue.\n// TODO: Consider doing the processing in parallel. This will require a little thought\n// to make sure that we don't end up processing the same object multiple times\n// concurrently.\nfunc (c *controller) processLoop(ctx context.Context) {\n\tuseBatchProcess := false\n\tbatchQueue, ok := c.config.Queue.(QueueWithBatch)\n\tif ok && c.config.ProcessBatch != nil && clientgofeaturegate.FeatureGates().Enabled(clientgofeaturegate.InOrderInformersBatchProcess) {\n\t\tuseBatchProcess = true\n\t}\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tvar err error\n\t\t\tif useBatchProcess {\n\t\t\t\terr = batchQueue.PopBatch(c.config.ProcessBatch, PopProcessFunc(c.config.Process))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// otherwise fallback to non-batch process behavior\n\t\t\t\t_, err = c.config.Pop(PopProcessFunc(c.config.Process))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, ErrFIFOClosed) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// ResourceEventHandler can handle notifications for events that\n// happen to a resource. The events are informational only, so you\n// can't return an error.  The handlers MUST NOT modify the objects\n// received; this concerns not only the top level of structure but all\n// the data structures reachable from it.\n//   - OnAdd is called when an object is added.\n//   - OnUpdate is called when an object is modified. Note that oldObj is the\n//     last known state of the object-- it is possible that several changes\n//     were combined together, so you can't use this to see every single\n//     change. OnUpdate is also called when a re-list happens, and it will\n//     get called even if nothing changed. This is useful for periodically\n//     evaluating or syncing something.\n//   - OnDelete will get the final state of the item if it is known, otherwise\n//     it will get an object of type DeletedFinalStateUnknown. This can\n//     happen if the watch is closed and misses the delete event and we don't\n//     notice the deletion until the subsequent re-list.\ntype ResourceEventHandler interface {\n\tOnAdd(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool)\n\tOnUpdate(oldObj, newObj interface{})\n\tOnDelete(obj interface{})\n}\n\n// ResourceEventHandlerFuncs is an adaptor to let you easily specify as many or\n// as few of the notification functions as you want while still implementing\n// ResourceEventHandler.  This adapter does not remove the prohibition against\n// modifying the objects.\n//\n// See ResourceEventHandlerDetailedFuncs if your use needs to propagate\n// HasSynced.\ntype ResourceEventHandlerFuncs struct {\n\tAddFunc    func(obj interface{})\n\tUpdateFunc func(oldObj, newObj interface{})\n\tDeleteFunc func(obj interface{})\n}\n\n// OnAdd calls AddFunc if it's not nil.\nfunc (r ResourceEventHandlerFuncs) OnAdd(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool) {\n\tif r.AddFunc != nil {\n\t\tr.AddFunc(obj)\n\t}\n}\n\n// OnUpdate calls UpdateFunc if it's not nil.\nfunc (r ResourceEventHandlerFuncs) OnUpdate(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\n\tif r.UpdateFunc != nil {\n\t\tr.UpdateFunc(oldObj, newObj)\n\t}\n}\n\n// OnDelete calls DeleteFunc if it's not nil.\nfunc (r ResourceEventHandlerFuncs) OnDelete(obj interface{}) {\n\tif r.DeleteFunc != nil {\n\t\tr.DeleteFunc(obj)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ResourceEventHandlerDetailedFuncs is exactly like ResourceEventHandlerFuncs\n// except its AddFunc accepts the isInInitialList parameter, for propagating\n// HasSynced.\ntype ResourceEventHandlerDetailedFuncs struct {\n\tAddFunc    func(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool)\n\tUpdateFunc func(oldObj, newObj interface{})\n\tDeleteFunc func(obj interface{})\n}\n\n// OnAdd calls AddFunc if it's not nil.\nfunc (r ResourceEventHandlerDetailedFuncs) OnAdd(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool) {\n\tif r.AddFunc != nil {\n\t\tr.AddFunc(obj, isInInitialList)\n\t}\n}\n\n// OnUpdate calls UpdateFunc if it's not nil.\nfunc (r ResourceEventHandlerDetailedFuncs) OnUpdate(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\n\tif r.UpdateFunc != nil {\n\t\tr.UpdateFunc(oldObj, newObj)\n\t}\n}\n\n// OnDelete calls DeleteFunc if it's not nil.\nfunc (r ResourceEventHandlerDetailedFuncs) OnDelete(obj interface{}) {\n\tif r.DeleteFunc != nil {\n\t\tr.DeleteFunc(obj)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FilteringResourceEventHandler applies the provided filter to all events coming\n// in, ensuring the appropriate nested handler method is invoked. An object\n// that starts passing the filter after an update is considered an add, and an\n// object that stops passing the filter after an update is considered a delete.\n// Like the handlers, the filter MUST NOT modify the objects it is given.\ntype FilteringResourceEventHandler struct {\n\tFilterFunc func(obj interface{}) bool\n\tHandler    ResourceEventHandler\n}\n\n// OnAdd calls the nested handler only if the filter succeeds\nfunc (r FilteringResourceEventHandler) OnAdd(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool) {\n\tif !r.FilterFunc(obj) {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tr.Handler.OnAdd(obj, isInInitialList)\n}\n\n// OnUpdate ensures the proper handler is called depending on whether the filter matches\nfunc (r FilteringResourceEventHandler) OnUpdate(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\n\tnewer := r.FilterFunc(newObj)\n\tolder := r.FilterFunc(oldObj)\n\tswitch {\n\tcase newer && older:\n\t\tr.Handler.OnUpdate(oldObj, newObj)\n\tcase newer && !older:\n\t\tr.Handler.OnAdd(newObj, false)\n\tcase !newer && older:\n\t\tr.Handler.OnDelete(oldObj)\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// do nothing\n\t}\n}\n\n// OnDelete calls the nested handler only if the filter succeeds\nfunc (r FilteringResourceEventHandler) OnDelete(obj interface{}) {\n\tif !r.FilterFunc(obj) {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tr.Handler.OnDelete(obj)\n}\n\n// DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc checks for\n// DeletedFinalStateUnknown objects before calling\n// MetaNamespaceKeyFunc.\nfunc DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(obj interface{}) (string, error) {\n\tif d, ok := obj.(DeletedFinalStateUnknown); ok {\n\t\treturn d.Key, nil\n\t}\n\treturn MetaNamespaceKeyFunc(obj)\n}\n\n// DeletionHandlingObjectToName checks for\n// DeletedFinalStateUnknown objects before calling\n// ObjectToName.\nfunc DeletionHandlingObjectToName(obj interface{}) (ObjectName, error) {\n\tif d, ok := obj.(DeletedFinalStateUnknown); ok {\n\t\treturn ParseObjectName(d.Key)\n\t}\n\treturn ObjectToName(obj)\n}\n\n// InformerOptions configure a Reflector.\ntype InformerOptions struct {\n\t// ListerWatcher implements List and Watch functions for the source of the resource\n\t// the informer will be informing about.\n\tListerWatcher ListerWatcher\n\n\t// ObjectType is an object of the type that informer is expected to receive.\n\tObjectType runtime.Object\n\n\t// Handler defines functions that should called on object mutations.\n\tHandler ResourceEventHandler\n\n\t// ResyncPeriod is the underlying Reflector's resync period. If non-zero, the store\n\t// is re-synced with that frequency - Modify events are delivered even if objects\n\t// didn't change.\n\t// This is useful for synchronizing objects that configure external resources\n\t// (e.g. configure cloud provider functionalities).\n\t// Optional - if unset, store resyncing is not happening periodically.\n\tResyncPeriod time.Duration\n\n\t// MinWatchTimeout, if set, will define the minimum timeout for watch requests send\n\t// to kube-apiserver. However, values lower than 5m will not be honored to avoid\n\t// negative performance impact on controlplane.\n\t// Optional - if unset a default value of 5m will be used.\n\tMinWatchTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// Indexers, if set, are the indexers for the received objects to optimize\n\t// certain queries.\n\t// Optional - if unset no indexes are maintained.\n\tIndexers Indexers\n\n\t// Transform function, if set, will be called on all objects before they will be\n\t// put into the Store and corresponding Add/Modify/Delete handlers will be invoked\n\t// for them.\n\t// Optional - if unset no additional transforming is happening.\n\tTransform TransformFunc\n}\n\n// NewInformerWithOptions returns a Store and a controller for populating the store\n// while also providing event notifications. You should only used the returned\n// Store for Get/List operations; Add/Modify/Deletes will cause the event\n// notifications to be faulty.\nfunc NewInformerWithOptions(options InformerOptions) (Store, Controller) {\n\tvar clientState Store\n\tif options.Indexers == nil {\n\t\tclientState = NewStore(DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\t} else {\n\t\tclientState = NewIndexer(DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc, options.Indexers)\n\t}\n\treturn clientState, newInformer(clientState, options, DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n}\n\n// NewInformer returns a Store and a controller for populating the store\n// while also providing event notifications. You should only used the returned\n// Store for Get/List operations; Add/Modify/Deletes will cause the event\n// notifications to be faulty.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - lw is list and watch functions for the source of the resource you want to\n//     be informed of.\n//   - objType is an object of the type that you expect to receive.\n//   - resyncPeriod: if non-zero, will re-list this often (you will get OnUpdate\n//     calls, even if nothing changed). Otherwise, re-list will be delayed as\n//     long as possible (until the upstream source closes the watch or times out,\n//     or you stop the controller).\n//   - h is the object you want notifications sent to.\n//\n// Deprecated: Use NewInformerWithOptions instead.\nfunc NewInformer(\n\tlw ListerWatcher,\n\tobjType runtime.Object,\n\tresyncPeriod time.Duration,\n\th ResourceEventHandler,\n) (Store, Controller) {\n\t// This will hold the client state, as we know it.\n\tclientState := NewStore(DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\n\toptions := InformerOptions{\n\t\tListerWatcher: lw,\n\t\tObjectType:    objType,\n\t\tHandler:       h,\n\t\tResyncPeriod:  resyncPeriod,\n\t}\n\treturn clientState, newInformer(clientState, options, DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n}\n\n// NewIndexerInformer returns an Indexer and a Controller for populating the index\n// while also providing event notifications. You should only used the returned\n// Index for Get/List operations; Add/Modify/Deletes will cause the event\n// notifications to be faulty.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - lw is list and watch functions for the source of the resource you want to\n//     be informed of.\n//   - objType is an object of the type that you expect to receive.\n//   - resyncPeriod: if non-zero, will re-list this often (you will get OnUpdate\n//     calls, even if nothing changed). Otherwise, re-list will be delayed as\n//     long as possible (until the upstream source closes the watch or times out,\n//     or you stop the controller).\n//   - h is the object you want notifications sent to.\n//   - indexers is the indexer for the received object type.\n//\n// Deprecated: Use NewInformerWithOptions instead.\nfunc NewIndexerInformer(\n\tlw ListerWatcher,\n\tobjType runtime.Object,\n\tresyncPeriod time.Duration,\n\th ResourceEventHandler,\n\tindexers Indexers,\n) (Indexer, Controller) {\n\t// This will hold the client state, as we know it.\n\tclientState := NewIndexer(DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc, indexers)\n\n\toptions := InformerOptions{\n\t\tListerWatcher: lw,\n\t\tObjectType:    objType,\n\t\tHandler:       h,\n\t\tResyncPeriod:  resyncPeriod,\n\t\tIndexers:      indexers,\n\t}\n\treturn clientState, newInformer(clientState, options, DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n}\n\n// NewTransformingInformer returns a Store and a controller for populating\n// the store while also providing event notifications. You should only used\n// the returned Store for Get/List operations; Add/Modify/Deletes will cause\n// the event notifications to be faulty.\n// The given transform function will be called on all objects before they will\n// put into the Store and corresponding Add/Modify/Delete handlers will\n// be invoked for them.\n//\n// Deprecated: Use NewInformerWithOptions instead.\nfunc NewTransformingInformer(\n\tlw ListerWatcher,\n\tobjType runtime.Object,\n\tresyncPeriod time.Duration,\n\th ResourceEventHandler,\n\ttransformer TransformFunc,\n) (Store, Controller) {\n\t// This will hold the client state, as we know it.\n\tclientState := NewStore(DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\n\toptions := InformerOptions{\n\t\tListerWatcher: lw,\n\t\tObjectType:    objType,\n\t\tHandler:       h,\n\t\tResyncPeriod:  resyncPeriod,\n\t\tTransform:     transformer,\n\t}\n\treturn clientState, newInformer(clientState, options, DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n}\n\n// NewTransformingIndexerInformer returns an Indexer and a controller for\n// populating the index while also providing event notifications. You should\n// only used the returned Index for Get/List operations; Add/Modify/Deletes\n// will cause the event notifications to be faulty.\n// The given transform function will be called on all objects before they will\n// be put into the Index and corresponding Add/Modify/Delete handlers will\n// be invoked for them.\n//\n// Deprecated: Use NewInformerWithOptions instead.\nfunc NewTransformingIndexerInformer(\n\tlw ListerWatcher,\n\tobjType runtime.Object,\n\tresyncPeriod time.Duration,\n\th ResourceEventHandler,\n\tindexers Indexers,\n\ttransformer TransformFunc,\n) (Indexer, Controller) {\n\t// This will hold the client state, as we know it.\n\tclientState := NewIndexer(DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc, indexers)\n\n\toptions := InformerOptions{\n\t\tListerWatcher: lw,\n\t\tObjectType:    objType,\n\t\tHandler:       h,\n\t\tResyncPeriod:  resyncPeriod,\n\t\tIndexers:      indexers,\n\t\tTransform:     transformer,\n\t}\n\treturn clientState, newInformer(clientState, options, DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n}\n\n// Multiplexes updates in the form of a list of Deltas into a Store, and informs\n// a given handler of events OnUpdate, OnAdd, OnDelete\nfunc processDeltas(\n\t// Object which receives event notifications from the given deltas\n\thandler ResourceEventHandler,\n\tclientState Store,\n\tdeltas Deltas,\n\tisInInitialList bool,\n\tkeyFunc KeyFunc,\n) error {\n\t// from oldest to newest\n\tfor _, d := range deltas {\n\t\tobj := d.Object\n\n\t\tswitch d.Type {\n\t\tcase ReplacedAll:\n\t\t\tinfo, ok := obj.(ReplacedAllInfo)\n\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ReplacedAll did not contain ReplacedAllInfo: %T\", obj)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := processReplacedAllInfo(handler, info, clientState, isInInitialList, keyFunc); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase SyncAll:\n\t\t\t_, ok := obj.(SyncAllInfo)\n\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"SyncAll did not contain SyncAllInfo: %T\", obj)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tobjs := clientState.List()\n\t\t\tfor _, obj := range objs {\n\t\t\t\thandler.OnUpdate(obj, obj)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\tcase Sync, Replaced, Added, Updated:\n\t\t\tif old, exists, err := clientState.Get(obj); err == nil && exists {\n\t\t\t\tif err := clientState.Update(obj); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\thandler.OnUpdate(old, obj)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err := clientState.Add(obj); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\thandler.OnAdd(obj, isInInitialList)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase Deleted:\n\t\t\tif err := clientState.Delete(obj); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\thandler.OnDelete(obj)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// processDeltasInBatch applies a batch of Delta objects to the given Store and\n// notifies the ResourceEventHandler of add, update, or delete events.\n//\n// If the Store supports transactions (TransactionStore), all Deltas are applied\n// atomically in a single transaction and corresponding handler callbacks are\n// executed afterward. Otherwise, each Delta is processed individually.\n//\n// Returns an error if any Delta or transaction fails. For TransactionError,\n// only successful operations trigger callbacks.\nfunc processDeltasInBatch(\n\thandler ResourceEventHandler,\n\tclientState Store,\n\tdeltas []Delta,\n\tisInInitialList bool,\n\tkeyFunc KeyFunc,\n) error {\n\t// from oldest to newest\n\ttxns := make([]Transaction, 0)\n\tcallbacks := make([]func(), 0)\n\ttxnStore, txnSupported := clientState.(TransactionStore)\n\tif !txnSupported {\n\t\tvar errs []error\n\t\tfor _, delta := range deltas {\n\t\t\tif err := processDeltas(handler, clientState, Deltas{delta}, isInInitialList, keyFunc); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected error when handling deltas: %v\", errs)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// deltasList is a list of unique objects\n\tfor _, d := range deltas {\n\t\tobj := d.Object\n\t\tswitch d.Type {\n\t\tcase Sync, Replaced, Added, Updated:\n\t\t\t// it will only return one old object for each because items are unique\n\t\t\tif old, exists, err := clientState.Get(obj); err == nil && exists {\n\t\t\t\ttxn := Transaction{\n\t\t\t\t\tType:   TransactionTypeUpdate,\n\t\t\t\t\tObject: obj,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\ttxns = append(txns, txn)\n\t\t\t\tcallbacks = append(callbacks, func() {\n\t\t\t\t\thandler.OnUpdate(old, obj)\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\ttxn := Transaction{\n\t\t\t\t\tType:   TransactionTypeAdd,\n\t\t\t\t\tObject: obj,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\ttxns = append(txns, txn)\n\t\t\t\tcallbacks = append(callbacks, func() {\n\t\t\t\t\thandler.OnAdd(obj, isInInitialList)\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase Deleted:\n\t\t\ttxn := Transaction{\n\t\t\t\tType:   TransactionTypeDelete,\n\t\t\t\tObject: obj,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttxns = append(txns, txn)\n\t\t\tcallbacks = append(callbacks, func() {\n\t\t\t\thandler.OnDelete(obj)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"Delta type %s is not supported in batch processing\", d.Type)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\terr := txnStore.Transaction(txns...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// if txn had error, only execute the callbacks for the successful ones\n\t\tfor _, i := range err.SuccessfulIndices {\n\t\t\tif i < len(callbacks) {\n\t\t\t\tcallbacks[i]()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// formatting the error so txns doesn't escape and keeps allocated in the stack.\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"not all items in the batch successfully processed: %s\", err.Error())\n\t}\n\tfor _, callback := range callbacks {\n\t\tcallback()\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc processReplacedAllInfo(handler ResourceEventHandler, info ReplacedAllInfo, clientState Store, isInInitialList bool, keyFunc KeyFunc) error {\n\tvar deletions []DeletedFinalStateUnknown\n\ttype replacement struct {\n\t\toldObj interface{}\n\t\tnewObj interface{}\n\t}\n\treplacements := make([]replacement, 0, len(info.Objects))\n\n\terr := reconcileReplacement(nil, clientState, info.Objects, keyFunc,\n\t\tfunc(obj DeletedFinalStateUnknown) error {\n\t\t\tdeletions = append(deletions, obj)\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t\tfunc(obj interface{}) error {\n\t\t\t// This behavior matches processDeltas handling of Replace deltas\n\t\t\tif old, exists, err := clientState.Get(obj); err == nil && exists {\n\t\t\t\treplacements = append(replacements, replacement{newObj: obj, oldObj: old})\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\treplacements = append(replacements, replacement{newObj: obj})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Replace the client state first so the store reflects the events handlers are given\n\tif err := clientState.Replace(info.Objects, info.ResourceVersion); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Processing all deletions first matches behavior of RealFIFO#Replace\n\tfor _, objToDelete := range deletions {\n\t\thandler.OnDelete(objToDelete)\n\t}\n\t// Processing adds/updates in order observed by reconcileReplacement matches behavior of RealFIFO#Replace\n\tfor _, r := range replacements {\n\t\tif r.oldObj != nil {\n\t\t\thandler.OnUpdate(r.oldObj, r.newObj)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\thandler.OnAdd(r.newObj, isInInitialList)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// newInformer returns a controller for populating the store while also\n// providing event notifications.\n//\n// Parameters\n//   - clientState is the store you want to populate\n//   - options contain the options to configure the controller\nfunc newInformer(clientState Store, options InformerOptions, keyFunc KeyFunc) Controller {\n\t// This will hold incoming changes. Note how we pass clientState in as a\n\t// KeyLister, that way resync operations will result in the correct set\n\t// of update/delete deltas.\n\n\tfifo := newQueueFIFO(clientState, options.Transform)\n\n\tcfg := &Config{\n\t\tQueue:            fifo,\n\t\tListerWatcher:    options.ListerWatcher,\n\t\tObjectType:       options.ObjectType,\n\t\tFullResyncPeriod: options.ResyncPeriod,\n\t\tMinWatchTimeout:  options.MinWatchTimeout,\n\n\t\tProcess: func(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool) error {\n\t\t\tif deltas, ok := obj.(Deltas); ok {\n\t\t\t\treturn processDeltas(options.Handler, clientState, deltas, isInInitialList, keyFunc)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn errors.New(\"object given as Process argument is not Deltas\")\n\t\t},\n\t\tProcessBatch: func(deltaList []Delta, isInInitialList bool) error {\n\t\t\treturn processDeltasInBatch(options.Handler, clientState, deltaList, isInInitialList, keyFunc)\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\treturn New(cfg)\n}\n\nfunc newQueueFIFO(clientState Store, transform TransformFunc) Queue {\n\tif clientgofeaturegate.FeatureGates().Enabled(clientgofeaturegate.InOrderInformers) {\n\t\toptions := RealFIFOOptions{\n\t\t\tKeyFunction: MetaNamespaceKeyFunc,\n\t\t\tTransformer: transform,\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If atomic events are enabled, unset clientState in the case of atomic events as we cannot pass a\n\t\t// store to an atomic fifo.\n\t\tif clientgofeaturegate.FeatureGates().Enabled(clientgofeaturegate.AtomicFIFO) {\n\t\t\toptions.AtomicEvents = true\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\toptions.KnownObjects = clientState\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn NewRealFIFOWithOptions(options)\n\t} else {\n\t\treturn NewDeltaFIFOWithOptions(DeltaFIFOOptions{\n\t\t\tKnownObjects:          clientState,\n\t\t\tEmitDeltaTypeReplaced: true,\n\t\t\tTransformer:           transform,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n", "numLines": 823, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 823}}

    57→// ReflectorStore is the subset of cache.Store that the reflector uses
    58→type ReflectorStore interface {
    59→	// Add adds the given object to the accumulator associated with the given object's key
    60→	Add(obj interface{}) error
    61→
    62→	// Update updates the given object in the accumulator associated with the given object's key
    63→	Update(obj interface{}) error
    64→
    65→	// Delete deletes the given object from the accumulator associated with the given object's key
    66→	Delete(obj interface{}) error
    67→
    68→	// Replace will delete the contents of the store, using instead the
    69→	// given list. Store takes ownership of the list, you should not reference
    70→	// it after calling this function.
    71→	Replace([]interface{}, string) error
    72→
    73→	// Resync is meaningless in the terms appearing here but has
    74→	// meaning in some implementations that have non-trivial
    75→	// additional behavior (e.g., DeltaFIFO).
    76→	Resync() error
    77→}
    78→
    79→// TransformingStore is an optional interface that can be implemented by the provided store.
    80→// If implemented on the provided store reflector will use the same transformer in its internal stores.
    81→type TransformingStore interface {
    82→	ReflectorStore
    83→	Transformer() TransformFunc
    84→}

    86→// Reflector watches a specified resource and causes all changes to be reflected in the given store.
    87→type Reflector struct {
    88→	// name identifies this reflector. By default, it will be a file:line if possible.
    89→	name string
    90→	// The name of the type we expect to place in the store. The name
    91→	// will be the stringification of expectedGVK if provided, and the
    92→	// stringification of expectedType otherwise. It is for display
    93→	// only, and should not be used for parsing or comparison.
    94→	typeDescription string
    95→	// An example object of the type we expect to place in the store.
    96→	// Only the type needs to be right, except that when that is
    97→	// `unstructured.Unstructured` the object's `"apiVersion"` and
    98→	// `"kind"` must also be right.
    99→	expectedType reflect.Type
   100→	// The GVK of the object we expect to place in the store if unstructured.
   101→	expectedGVK *schema.GroupVersionKind
   102→	// The destination to sync up with the watch source
   103→	store ReflectorStore
   104→	// listerWatcher is used to perform lists and watches.
   105→	listerWatcher ListerWatcherWithContext
   106→	// backoff manages backoff of ListWatch
   107→	backoffManager wait.BackoffManager
   108→	resyncPeriod   time.Duration
   109→	// minWatchTimeout defines the minimum timeout for watch requests.
   110→	minWatchTimeout time.Duration
   111→	// clock allows tests to manipulate time
   112→	clock clock.Clock
   113→	// paginatedResult defines whether pagination should be forced for list calls.
   114→	// It is set based on the result of the initial list call.
   115→	paginatedResult bool
   116→	// lastSyncResourceVersion is the resource version token last
   117→	// observed when doing a sync with the underlying store
   118→	// it is thread safe, but not synchronized with the underlying store
   119→	lastSyncResourceVersion string
   120→	// isLastSyncResourceVersionUnavailable is true if the previous list or watch request with
   121→	// lastSyncResourceVersion failed with an "expired" or "too large resource version" error.
   122→	isLastSyncResourceVersionUnavailable bool
   123→	// lastSyncResourceVersionMutex guards read/write access to lastSyncResourceVersion
   124→	lastSyncResourceVersionMutex sync.RWMutex
   125→	// Called whenever the ListAndWatch drops the connection with an error.
   126→	watchErrorHandler WatchErrorHandlerWithContext
   127→	// WatchListPageSize is the requested chunk size of initial and resync watch lists.
   128→	// If unset, for consistent reads (RV="") or reads that opt-into arbitrarily old data
   129→	// (RV="0") it will default to pager.PageSize, for the rest (RV != "" && RV != "0")
   130→	// it will turn off pagination to allow serving them from watch cache.
   131→	// NOTE: It should be used carefully as paginated lists are always served directly from
   132→	// etcd, which is significantly less efficient and may lead to serious performance and
   133→	// scalability problems.
   134→	WatchListPageSize int64
   135→	// ShouldResync is invoked periodically and whenever it returns `true` the Store's Resync operation is invoked
   136→	ShouldResync func() bool
   137→	// MaxInternalErrorRetryDuration defines how long we should retry internal errors returned by watch.
   138→	MaxInternalErrorRetryDuration time.Duration
   139→	// useWatchList if turned on instructs the reflector to open a stream to bring data from the API server.
   140→	// Streaming has the primary advantage of using fewer server's resources to fetch data.
   141→	//
   142→	// The old behaviour establishes a LIST request which gets data in chunks.
   143→	// Paginated list is less efficient and depending on the actual size of objects
   144→	// might result in an increased memory consumption of the APIServer.
   145→	//
   146→	// See https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/3157-watch-list#design-details
   147→	useWatchList bool
   148→}

it is random in [minWatchTimeout, 2*minWatchTimeout].\nvar defaultMinWatchTimeout = 5 * time.Minute\n\n// ReflectorStore is the subset of cache.Store that the reflector uses\ntype ReflectorStore interface {\n\t// Add adds the given object to the accumulator associated with the given object's key\n\tAdd(obj interface{}) error\n\n\t// Update updates the given object in the accumulator associated with the given object's key\n\tUpdate(obj interface{}) error\n\n\t// Delete deletes the given object from the accumulator associated with the given object's key\n\tDelete(obj interface{}) error\n\n\t// Replace will delete the contents of the store, using instead the\n\t// given list. Store takes ownership of the list, you should not reference\n\t// it after calling this function.\n\tReplace([]interface{}, string) error\n\n\t// Resync is meaningless in the terms appearing here but has\n\t// meaning in some implementations that have non-trivial\n\t// additional behavior (e.g., DeltaFIFO).\n\tResync() error\n}\n\n// TransformingStore is an optional interface that can be implemented by the provided store.\n// If implemented on the provided store reflector will use the same transformer in its internal stores.\ntype TransformingStore interface {\n\tReflectorStore\n\tTransformer() TransformFunc\n}\n\n// Reflector watches a specified resource and causes all changes to be reflected in the given store.\ntype Reflector struct {\n\t// name identifies this reflector. By default, it will be a file:line if possible.\n\tname string\n\t// The name of the type we expect to place in the store. The name\n\t// will be the stringification of expectedGVK if provided, and the\n\t// stringification of expectedType otherwise. It is for display\n\t// only, and should not be used for parsing or comparison.\n\ttypeDescription string\n\t// An example object of the type we expect to place in the store.\n\t// Only the type needs to be right, except that when that is\n\t// `unstructured.Unstructured` the object's `\"apiVersion\"` and\n\t// `\"kind\"` must also be right.\n\texpectedType reflect.Type\n\t// The GVK of the object we expect to place in the store if unstructured.\n\texpectedGVK *schema.GroupVersionKind\n\t// The destination to sync up with the watch source\n\tstore ReflectorStore\n\t// listerWatcher is used to perform lists and watches.\n\tlisterWatcher ListerWatcherWithContext\n\t// backoff manages backoff of ListWatch\n\tbackoffManager wait.BackoffManager\n\tresyncPeriod   time.Duration\n\t// minWatchTimeout defines the minimum timeout for watch requests.\n\tminWatchTimeout time.Duration\n\t// clock allows tests to manipulate time\n\tclock clock.Clock\n\t// paginatedResult defines whether pagination should be forced for list calls.\n\t// It is set based on the result of the initial list call.\n\tpaginatedResult bool\n\t// lastSyncResourceVersion is the resource version token last\n\t// observed when doing a sync with the underlying store\n\t// it is thread safe, but not synchronized with the underlying store\n\tlastSyncResourceVersion string\n\t// isLastSyncResourceVersionUnavailable is true if the previous list or watch request with\n\t// lastSyncResourceVersion failed with an \"expired\" or \"too large resource version\" error.\n\tisLastSyncResourceVersionUnavailable bool\n\t// lastSyncResourceVersionMutex guards read/write access to lastSyncResourceVersion\n\tlastSyncResourceVersionMutex sync.RWMutex\n\t// Called whenever the ListAndWatch drops the connection with an error.\n\twatchErrorHandler WatchErrorHandlerWithContext\n\t// WatchListPageSize is the requested chunk size of initial and resync watch lists.\n\t// If unset, for consistent reads (RV=\"\") or reads that opt-into arbitrarily old data\n\t// (RV=\"0\") it will default to pager.PageSize, for the rest (RV != \"\" && RV != \"0\")\n\t// it will turn off pagination to allow serving them from watch cache.\n\t// NOTE: It should be used carefully as paginated lists are always served directly from\n\t// etcd, which is significantly less efficient and may lead to serious performance and\n\t// scalability problems.\n\tWatchListPageSize int64\n\t// ShouldResync is invoked periodically and whenever it returns `true` the Store's Resync operation is invoked\n\tShouldResync func() bool\n\t// MaxInternalErrorRetryDuration defines how long we should retry internal errors returned by watch.\n\tMaxInternalErrorRetryDuration time.Duration\n\t// useWatchList if turned on instructs the reflector to open a stream to bring data from the API server.\n\t// Streaming has the primary advantage of using fewer server's resources to fetch data.\n\t//\n\t// The old behaviour establishes a LIST request which gets data in chunks.\n\t// Paginated list is less efficient and depending on the actual size of objects\n\t// might result in an increased memory consumption of the APIServer.\n\t//\n\t// See https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/3157-watch-list#design-details\n\tuseWatchList bool\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reflector) Name() string {", "numLines": 150, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 1253}}

150→	AddEventHandler(handler ResourceEventHandler) (ResourceEventHandlerRegistration, error)
   151→	// AddEventHandlerWithResyncPeriod adds an event handler to the
   152→	// shared informer with the requested resync period; zero means
   153→	// this handler does not care about resyncs.  The resync operation
   154→	// consists of delivering to the handler an update notification
   155→	// for every object in the informer's local cache; it does not add
   156→	// any interactions with the authoritative storage.  Some
   157→	// informers do no resyncs at all, not even for handlers added
   158→	// with a non-zero resyncPeriod.  For an informer that does
   159→	// resyncs, and for each handler that requests resyncs, that
   160→	// informer develops a nominal resync period that is no shorter
   161→	// than the requested period but may be longer.  The actual time
   162→	// between any two resyncs may be longer than the nominal period
   163→	// because the implementation takes time to do work and there may
   164→	// be competing load and scheduling noise.
   165→	// It returns a registration handle for the handler that can be used to remove
   166→	// the handler again and an error if the handler cannot be added.
   167→	//
   168→	// Contextual logging: AddEventHandlerWithOptions together with a logger in the options should be used instead of AddEventHandlerWithResyncPeriod in code which supports contextual logging.
   169→	AddEventHandlerWithResyncPeriod(handler ResourceEventHandler, resyncPeriod time.Duration) (ResourceEventHandlerRegistration, error)
   170→	// AddEventHandlerWithOptions is a variant of AddEventHandlerWithResyncPeriod where
   171→	// all optional parameters are passed in a struct.
   172→	AddEventHandlerWithOptions(handler ResourceEventHandler, options HandlerOptions) (ResourceEventHandlerRegistration, error)
   173→	// RemoveEventHandler removes a formerly added event handler given by
   174→	// its registration handle.
   175→	// This function is guaranteed to be idempotent, and thread-safe.
   176→	RemoveEventHandler(handle ResourceEventHandlerRegistration) error
   177→	// GetStore returns the informer's local cache as a Store.
   178→	GetStore() Store
   179→	// GetController is deprecated, it does nothing useful
   180→	GetController() Controller
   181→	// Run starts and runs the shared informer, returning after it stops.
   182→	// The informer will be stopped when stopCh is closed.
   183→	//
   184→	// Contextual logging: RunWithContext should be used instead of Run in code which uses contextual logging.
   185→	Run(stopCh <-chan struct{})
   186→	// RunWithContext starts and runs the shared informer, returning after it stops.
   187→	// The informer will be stopped when the context is canceled.
   188→	RunWithContext(ctx context.Context)
   189→	// HasSynced returns true if the shared informer's store has been
   190→	// informed by at least one full LIST of the authoritative state
   191→	// of the informer's object collection.  This is unrelated to "resync".
   192→	//
   193→	// Note that this doesn't tell you if an individual handler is synced!!
   194→	// For that, please call HasSynced on the handle returned by
   195→	// AddEventHandler.
   196→	HasSynced() bool
   197→	// LastSyncResourceVersion is the resource version observed when last synced with the underlying
   198→	// store. The value returned is not synchronized with access to the underlying store and is not
   199→	// thread-safe.
   200→	LastSyncResourceVersion() string
   201→
   202→	// The WatchErrorHandler is called whenever ListAndWatch drops the
   203→	// connection with an error. After calling this handler, the informer
   204→	// will backoff and retry.
   205→	//
   206→	// The default implementation looks at the error type and tries to log
   207→	// the error message at an appropriate level.
   208→	//
   209→	// There's only one handler, so if you call this multiple times, last one
   210→	// wins; calling after the informer has been started returns an error.
   211→	//
   212→	// The handler is intended for visibility, not to e.g. pause the consumers.
   213→	// The handler should return quickly - any expensive processing should be
   214→	// offloaded.
   215→	//
   216→	// Contextual logging: SetWatchErrorHandlerWithContext should be used instead of SetWatchErrorHandler in code which supports contextual logging.
   217→	SetWatchErrorHandler(handler WatchErrorHandler) error
   218→
   219→	// SetWatchErrorHandlerWithContext is a variant of SetWatchErrorHandler where
   220→	// the handler is passed an additional context parameter.
   221→	SetWatchErrorHandlerWithContext(handler WatchErrorHandlerWithContext) error
   222→
   223→	// The TransformFunc is called for each object which is about to be stored.
   224→	//
   225→	// This function is intended for you to take the opportunity to
   226→	// remove, transform, or normalize fields. One use case is to strip unused
   227→	// metadata fields out of objects to save on RAM cost.
   228→	//
   229→	// Must be set before starting the informer.
   230→	//
   231→	// Please see the comment on TransformFunc for more details.
   232→	SetTransform(handler TransformFunc) error
   233→
   234→	// IsStopped reports whether the informer has already been stopped.
   235→	// Adding event handlers to already stopped informers is not possible.
   236→	// An informer already stopped will never be started again.
   237→	IsStopped() bool
   238→}
   239→
   240→// Opaque interface representing the registration of ResourceEventHandler for
   241→// a SharedInformer. Must be supplied back to the same SharedInformer's
   242→// `RemoveEventHandler` to unregister the handlers.
   243→//
   244→// Also used to tell if the handler is synced (has had all items in the initial
   245→// list delivered).
   246→type ResourceEventHandlerRegistration interface {
   247→	// HasSynced reports if both the parent has synced and all pre-sync
   248→	// events have been delivered.
   249→	HasSynced() bool
   250→}
   251→
   252→// Optional configuration options for [SharedInformer.AddEventHandlerWithOptions].
   253→// May be left empty.
   254→type HandlerOptions struct {
   255→	// Logger overrides the default klog.Background() logger.
   256→	Logger *klog.Logger
   257→
   258→	// ResyncPeriod requests a certain resync period from an informer. Zero
   259→	// means the handler does not care about resyncs. Not all informers do
   260→	// resyncs, even if requested. See
   261→	// [SharedInformer.AddEventHandlerWithResyncPeriod] for details.
   262→	//
   263→	// If nil, the default resync period of the shared informer is used.
   264→	ResyncPeriod *time.Duration
   265→}
   266→
   267→// SharedIndexInformer provides add and get Indexers ability based on SharedInformer.
   268→type SharedIndexInformer interface {
   269→	SharedInformer
   270→	AddIndexers(indexers Indexers) error
   271→	GetIndexer() Indexer
   272→}
   273→
   274→// NewSharedInformer creates a new instance for the ListerWatcher. See NewSharedIndexInformerWithOptions for full details.
   275→func NewSharedInformer(lw ListerWatcher, exampleObject runtime.Object, defaultEventHandlerResyncPeriod time.Duration) SharedInformer {
   276→	return NewSharedIndexInformer(lw, exampleObject, defaultEventHandlerResyncPeriod, Indexers{})
   277→}
   278→
   279→// NewSharedIndexInformer creates a new instance for the ListerWatcher and specified Indexers. See
   280→// NewSharedIndexInformerWithOptions for full details.
   281→func NewSharedIndexInformer(lw ListerWatcher, exampleObject runtime.Object, defaultEventHandlerResyncPeriod time.Duration, indexers Indexers) SharedIndexInformer {
   282→	return NewSharedIndexInformerWithOptions(
   283→		lw,
   284→		exampleObject,
   285→		SharedIndexInformerOptions{
   286→			ResyncPeriod: defaultEventHandlerResyncPeriod,
   287→			Indexers:     indexers,
   288→		},
   289→	)
   290→}
   291→
   292→// NewSharedIndexInformerWithOptions creates a new instance for the ListerWatcher.
   293→// The created informer will not do resyncs if options.ResyncPeriod is zero.  Otherwise: for each
   294→// handler that with a non-zero requested resync period, whether added
   295→// before or after the informer starts, the nominal resync period is
   296→// the requested resync period rounded up to a multiple of the
   297→// informer's resync checking period.  Such an informer's resync
   298→// checking period is established when the informer starts running,
   299→// and is the maximum of (a) the minimum of the resync periods
   300→// requested before the informer starts and the
   301→// options.ResyncPeriod given here and (b) the constant
   302→// `minimumResyncPeriod` defined in this file.
   303→func NewSharedIndexInformerWithOptions(lw ListerWatcher, exampleObject runtime.Object, options SharedIndexInformerOptions) SharedIndexInformer {
   304→	realClock := &clock.RealClock{}
   305→
   306→	processor := &sharedProcessor{clock: realClock}
   307→	processor.listenersRCond = sync.NewCond(processor.listenersLock.RLocker())
   308→
   309→	return &sharedIndexInformer{
   310→		indexer:                         NewIndexer(DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc, options.Indexers),
   311→		processor:                       processor,
   312→		listerWatcher:                   lw,
   313→		objectType:                      exampleObject,
   314→		objectDescription:               options.ObjectDescription,
   315→		resyncCheckPeriod:               options.ResyncPeriod,
   316→		defaultEventHandlerResyncPeriod: options.ResyncPeriod,
   317→		clock:                           realClock,
   318→		cacheMutationDetector:           NewCacheMutationDetector(fmt.Sprintf("%T", exampleObject)),
   319→		keyFunc:                         DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc,
   320→	}
   321→}
   322→
   323→// SharedIndexInformerOptions configures a sharedIndexInformer.
   324→type SharedIndexInformerOptions struct {
   325→	// ResyncPeriod is the default event handler resync period and resync check
   326→	// period. If unset/unspecified, these are defaulted to 0 (do not resync).
   327→	ResyncPeriod time.Duration
   328→
   329→	// Indexers is the sharedIndexInformer's indexers. If unset/unspecified, no indexers are configured.
   330→	Indexers Indexers
   331→
   332→	// ObjectDescription is the sharedIndexInformer's object description. This is passed through to the
   333→	// underlying Reflector's type description.
   334→	ObjectDescription string
   335→}
   336→
   337→// InformerSynced is a function that can be used to determine if an informer has synced.  This is useful for determining if caches have synced.
   338→type InformerSynced func() bool
   339→
   340→const (
   341→	// syncedPollPeriod controls how often you look at the status of your sync funcs
   342→	syncedPollPeriod = 100 * time.Millisecond
   343→
   344→	// initialBufferSize is the initial number of event notifications that can be buffered.
   345→	initialBufferSize = 1024
   346→)
   347→
   348→// WaitForNamedCacheSync is a wrapper around WaitForCacheSync that generates log messages
   349→// indicating that the caller identified by name is waiting for syncs, followed by
   350→// either a successful or failed sync.
   351→//
   352→// Contextual logging: WaitForNamedCacheSyncWithContext should be used instead of WaitForNamedCacheSync in code which supports contextual logging.
   353→func WaitForNamedCacheSync(controllerName string, stopCh <-chan struct{}, cacheSyncs ...InformerSynced) bool {
   354→	klog.Background().Info("Waiting for caches to sync", "controller", controllerName)
   355→
   356→	if !WaitForCacheSync(stopCh, cacheSyncs...) {
   357→		utilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(context.Background(), nil, "Unable to sync caches", "controller", controllerName)
   358→		return false
   359→	}
   360→
   361→	klog.Background().Info("Caches are synced", "controller", controllerName)
   362→	return true
   363→}
   364→
   365→// WaitForNamedCacheSyncWithContext is a wrapper around WaitForCacheSyncWithContext that generates log messages
   366→// indicating that the caller is waiting for syncs, followed by either a successful or failed sync.
   367→//
   368→// Contextual logging can be used to identify the caller in those log messages. The log level is zero,
   369→// the same as in [WaitForNamedCacheSync]. If this is too verbose, then store a logger with an increased
   370→// threshold in the context:
   371→//
   372→//	WaitForNamedCacheSyncWithContext(klog.NewContext(ctx, logger.V(5)), ...)
   373→func WaitForNamedCacheSyncWithContext(ctx context.Context, cacheSyncs ...InformerSynced) bool {
   374→	logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
   375→	logger.Info("Waiting for caches to sync")
   376→
   377→	if !WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), cacheSyncs...) {
   378→		utilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(ctx, nil, "Unable to sync caches")
   379→		return false
   380→	}
   381→
   382→	logger.Info("Caches are synced")
   383→	return true
   384→}
   385→
   386→// WaitForCacheSync waits for caches to populate.  It returns true if it was successful, false
   387→// if the controller should shutdown
   388→// callers should prefer WaitForNamedCacheSync()
   389→func WaitForCacheSync(stopCh <-chan struct{}, cacheSyncs ...InformerSynced) bool {
   390→	err := wait.PollImmediateUntil(syncedPollPeriod,
   391→		func() (bool, error) {
   392→			for _, syncFunc := range cacheSyncs {
   393→				if !syncFunc() {
   394→					return false, nil
   395→				}
   396→			}
   397→			return true, nil
   398→		},
   399→		stopCh)

150→func (r *Reflector) Name() string {

   158→// ResourceVersionUpdater is an interface that allows store implementation to
   159→// track the current resource version of the reflector. This is especially
   160→// important if storage bookmarks are enabled.
   161→type ResourceVersionUpdater interface {
   162→	// UpdateResourceVersion is called each time current resource version of the reflector
   163→	// is updated.
   164→	UpdateResourceVersion(resourceVersion string)

   167→// The WatchErrorHandler is called whenever ListAndWatch drops the
   168→// connection with an error. After calling this handler, the informer
   169→// will backoff and retry.
   170→//
   171→// The default implementation looks at the error type and tries to log
   172→// the error message at an appropriate level.
   173→//
   174→// Implementations of this handler may display the error message in other
   175→// ways. Implementations should return quickly - any expensive processing
   176→// should be offloaded.
   177→type WatchErrorHandler func(r *Reflector, err error)
   178→
   179→// The WatchErrorHandler is called whenever ListAndWatch drops the
   180→// connection with an error. After calling this handler, the informer
   181→// will backoff and retry.
   182→//
   183→// The default implementation looks at the error type and tries to log
   184→// the error message at an appropriate level.
   185→//
   186→// Implementations of this handler may display the error message in other
   187→// ways. Implementations should return quickly - any expensive processing
   188→// should be offloaded.
   189→type WatchErrorHandlerWithContext func(ctx context.Context, r *Reflector, err error)
   190→
   191→// DefaultWatchErrorHandler is the default implementation of WatchErrorHandlerWithContext.
   192→func DefaultWatchErrorHandler(ctx context.Context, r *Reflector, err error) {
   193→	switch {
   194→	case isExpiredError(err):
   195→		// Don't set LastSyncResourceVersionUnavailable - LIST call with ResourceVersion=RV already
   196→		// has a semantic that it returns data at least as fresh as provided RV.
   197→		// So first try to LIST with setting RV to resource version of last observed object.
   198→		klog.FromContext(ctx).V(4).Info("Watch closed", "reflector", r.name, "type", r.typeDescription, "err", err)
   199→	case err == io.EOF:
   200→		// watch closed normally
   201→	case err == io.ErrUnexpectedEOF:
   202→		klog.FromContext(ctx).V(1).Info("Watch closed with unexpected EOF", "reflector", r.name, "type", r.typeDescription, "err", err)
   203→	default:
   204→		utilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(ctx, err, "Failed to watch", "reflector", r.name, "type", r.typeDescription)
   205→	}
   206→}

   208→// NewNamespaceKeyedIndexerAndReflector creates an Indexer and a Reflector
   209→// The indexer is configured to key on namespace
   210→func NewNamespaceKeyedIndexerAndReflector(lw ListerWatcher, expectedType interface{}, resyncPeriod time.Duration) (indexer Indexer, reflector *Reflector) {
   211→	indexer = NewIndexer(MetaNamespaceKeyFunc, Indexers{NamespaceIndex: MetaNamespaceIndexFunc})
   212→	reflector = NewReflector(lw, expectedType, indexer, resyncPeriod)
   213→	return indexer, reflector
   214→}

   216→// NewReflector creates a new Reflector with its name defaulted to the closest source_file.go:line in the call stack
   217→// that is outside this package. See NewReflectorWithOptions for further information.
   218→func NewReflector(lw ListerWatcher, expectedType interface{}, store ReflectorStore, resyncPeriod time.Duration) *Reflector {
   219→	return NewReflectorWithOptions(lw, expectedType, store, ReflectorOptions{ResyncPeriod: resyncPeriod})
   220→}

   222→// NewNamedReflector creates a new Reflector with the specified name. See NewReflectorWithOptions for further
   223→// information.
   224→func NewNamedReflector(name string, lw ListerWatcher, expectedType interface{}, store ReflectorStore, resyncPeriod time.Duration) *Reflector {
   225→	return NewReflectorWithOptions(lw, expectedType, store, ReflectorOptions{Name: name, ResyncPeriod: resyncPeriod})
   226→}

   228→// ReflectorOptions configures a Reflector.
   229→type ReflectorOptions struct {
   230→	// Name is the Reflector's name. If unset/unspecified, the name defaults to the closest source_file.go:line
   231→	// in the call stack that is outside this package.
   232→	Name string
   233→
   234→	// TypeDescription is the Reflector's type description. If unset/unspecified, the type description is defaulted
   235→	// using the following rules: if the expectedType passed to NewReflectorWithOptions was nil, the type description is
   236→	// "<unspecified>". If the expectedType is an instance of *unstructured.Unstructured and its apiVersion and kind fields
   237→	// are set, the type description is the string encoding of those. Otherwise, the type description is set to the
   238→	// go type of expectedType..
   239→	TypeDescription string
   240→
   241→	// ResyncPeriod is the Reflector's resync period. If unset/unspecified, the resync period defaults to 0
   242→	// (do not resync).
   243→	ResyncPeriod time.Duration
   244→
   245→	// MinWatchTimeout, if non-zero, defines the minimum timeout for watch requests send to kube-apiserver.
   246→	// However, values lower than 5m will not be honored to avoid negative performance impact on controlplane.
   247→	MinWatchTimeout time.Duration
   248→
   249→	// Clock allows tests to control time. If unset defaults to clock.RealClock{}
   250→	Clock clock.Clock
   251→}
   252→
   253→// NewReflectorWithOptions creates a new Reflector object which will keep the
   254→// given store up to date with the server's contents for the given
   255→// resource. Reflector promises to only put things in the store that
   256→// have the type of expectedType, unless expectedType is nil. If
   257→// resyncPeriod is non-zero, then the reflector will periodically
   258→// consult its ShouldResync function to determine whether to invoke
   259→// the Store's Resync operation; `ShouldResync==nil` means always
   260→// "yes".  This enables you to use reflectors to periodically process
   261→// everything as well as incrementally processing the things that
   262→// change.
   263→func NewReflectorWithOptions(lw ListerWatcher, expectedType interface{}, store ReflectorStore, options ReflectorOptions) *Reflector {
   264→	reflectorClock := options.Clock
   265→	if reflectorClock == nil {
   266→		reflectorClock = clock.RealClock{}
   267→	}
   268→	minWatchTimeout := defaultMinWatchTimeout
   269→	if options.MinWatchTimeout > defaultMinWatchTimeout {
   270→		minWatchTimeout = options.MinWatchTimeout
   271→	}
   272→	r := &Reflector{
   273→		name:            options.Name,
   274→		resyncPeriod:    options.ResyncPeriod,
   275→		minWatchTimeout: minWatchTimeout,
   276→		typeDescription: options.TypeDescription,
   277→		listerWatcher:   ToListerWatcherWithContext(lw),
   278→		store:           store,
   279→		// We used to make the call every 1sec (1 QPS), the goal here is to achieve ~98% traffic reduction when
   280→		// API server is not healthy. With these parameters, backoff will stop at [30,60) sec interval which is
   281→		// 0.22 QPS. If we don't backoff for 2min, assume API server is healthy and we reset the backoff.
   282→		backoffManager:    wait.NewExponentialBackoffManager(800*time.Millisecond, 30*time.Second, 2*time.Minute, 2.0, 1.0, reflectorClock),
   283→		clock:             reflectorClock,
   284→		watchErrorHandler: WatchErrorHandlerWithContext(DefaultWatchErrorHandler),
   285→		expectedType:      reflect.TypeOf(expectedType),
   286→	}
   287→
   288→	if r.name == "" {
   289→		r.name = naming.GetNameFromCallsite(internalPackages...)
   290→	}
   291→
   292→	if r.typeDescription == "" {
   293→		r.typeDescription = getTypeDescriptionFromObject(expectedType)
   294→	}
   295→
   296→	if r.expectedGVK == nil {
   297→		r.expectedGVK = getExpectedGVKFromObject(expectedType)
   298→	}
   299→
   300→	r.useWatchList = clientfeatures.FeatureGates().Enabled(clientfeatures.WatchListClient)
   301→	if r.useWatchList && watchlist.DoesClientNotSupportWatchListSemantics(lw) {
   302→		// Using klog.TODO() here because switching to a caller-provided contextual logger
   303→		// would require an API change and updating all existing call sites.
   304→		klog.TODO().V(2).Info(
   305→			"The client used to build this informer/reflector doesn't support WatchList semantics. The feature will be disabled. This is expected in unit tests but not in production. For details, see the documentation of watchlist.DoesClientNotSupportWatchListSemantics().",
   306→			"feature", clientfeatures.WatchListClient,
   307→		)
   308→		r.useWatchList = false
   309→	}
   310→
   311→	return r
   312→}
   313→
   314→func getTypeDescriptionFromObject(expectedType interface{}) string {
   315→	if expectedType == nil {
   316→		return defaultExpectedTypeName
   317→	}
   318→
   319→	reflectDescription := reflect.TypeOf(expectedType).String()
   320→
   321→	obj, ok := expectedType.(*unstructured.Unstructured)
   322→	if !ok {
   323→		return reflectDescription
   324→	}
   325→
   326→	gvk := obj.GroupVersionKind()
   327→	if gvk.Empty() {
   328→		return reflectDescription
   329→	}
   330→
   331→	return gvk.String()
   332→}
   333→
   334→func getExpectedGVKFromObject(expectedType interface{}) *schema.GroupVersionKind {
   335→	obj, ok := expectedType.(*unstructured.Unstructured)
   336→	if !ok {
   337→		return nil
   338→	}
   339→
   340→	gvk := obj.GroupVersionKind()
   341→	if gvk.Empty() {
   342→		return nil
   343→	}
   344→
   345→	return &gvk
   346→}

    25→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets"
    26→
    27→	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    28→	utiltrace "k8s.io/utils/trace"
    29→)
    30→
    31→// DeltaFIFOOptions is the configuration parameters for DeltaFIFO. All are
    32→// optional.
    33→type DeltaFIFOOptions struct {
    34→
    35→	// KeyFunction is used to figure out what key an object should have. (It's
    36→	// exposed in the returned DeltaFIFO's KeyOf() method, with additional
    37→	// handling around deleted objects and queue state).
    38→	// Optional, the default is MetaNamespaceKeyFunc.
    39→	KeyFunction KeyFunc
    40→
    41→	// KnownObjects is expected to return a list of keys that the consumer of
    42→	// this queue "knows about". It is used to decide which items are missing
    43→	// when Replace() is called; 'Deleted' deltas are produced for the missing items.
    44→	// KnownObjects may be nil if you can tolerate missing deletions on Replace().
    45→	KnownObjects KeyListerGetter
    46→
    47→	// EmitDeltaTypeReplaced indicates that the queue consumer
    48→	// understands the Replaced DeltaType. Before the `Replaced` event type was
    49→	// added, calls to Replace() were handled the same as Sync(). For
    50→	// backwards-compatibility purposes, this is false by default.
    51→	// When true, `Replaced` events will be sent for items passed to a Replace() call.
    52→	// When false, `Sync` events will be sent instead.
    53→	EmitDeltaTypeReplaced bool
    54→
    55→	// If set, will be called for objects before enqueueing them. Please
    56→	// see the comment on TransformFunc for details.
    57→	Transformer TransformFunc
    58→
    59→	// If set, log output will go to this logger instead of klog.Background().
    60→	Logger *klog.Logger
    61→}
    62→
    63→// DeltaFIFO is like FIFO, but differs in two ways.  One is that the
    64→// accumulator associated with a given object's key is not that object
    65→// but rather a Deltas, which is a slice of Delta values for that
    66→// object.  Applying an object to a Deltas means to append a Delta
    67→// except when the potentially appended Delta is a Deleted and the
    68→// Deltas already ends with a Deleted.  In that case the Deltas does
    69→// not grow, although the terminal Deleted will be replaced by the new
    70→// Deleted if the older Deleted's object is a
    71→// DeletedFinalStateUnknown.
    72→//
    73→// The other difference is that DeltaFIFO has two additional ways that
    74→// an object can be applied to an accumulator: Replaced and Sync.
    75→// If EmitDeltaTypeReplaced is not set to true, Sync will be used in
    76→// replace events for backwards compatibility.  Sync is used for periodic
    77→// resync events.
    78→//
    79→// DeltaFIFO is a producer-consumer queue, where a Reflector is
    80→// intended to be the producer, and the consumer is whatever calls
    81→// the Pop() method.
    82→//
    83→// DeltaFIFO solves this use case:
    84→//   - You want to process every object change (delta) at most once.
    85→//   - When you process an object, you want to see everything
    86→//     that's happened to it since you last processed it.
    87→//   - You want to process the deletion of some of the objects.
    88→//   - You might want to periodically reprocess objects.
    89→//
    90→// DeltaFIFO's Pop(), Get(), and GetByKey() methods return
    91→// interface{} to satisfy the Store/Queue interfaces, but they
    92→// will always return an object of type Deltas. List() returns
    93→// the newest object from each accumulator in the FIFO.
    94→//
    95→// A DeltaFIFO's knownObjects KeyListerGetter provides the abilities
    96→// to list Store keys and to get objects by Store key.  The objects in
    97→// question are called "known objects" and this set of objects
    98→// modifies the behavior of the Delete, Replace, and Resync methods
    99→// (each in a different way).
   100→//
   101→// A note on threading: If you call Pop() in parallel from multiple
   102→// threads, you could end up with multiple threads processing slightly
   103→// different versions of the same object.
   104→type DeltaFIFO struct {
   105→	// lock/cond protects access to 'items' and 'queue'.
   106→	lock sync.RWMutex
   107→	cond sync.Cond
   108→
   109→	// `items` maps a key to a Deltas.
   110→	// Each such Deltas has at least one Delta.
   111→	items map[string]Deltas
   112→
   113→	// `queue` maintains FIFO order of keys for consumption in Pop().
   114→	// There are no duplicates in `queue`.
   115→	// A key is in `queue` if and only if it is in `items`.
   116→	queue []string
   117→
   118→	// populated is true if the first batch of items inserted by Replace() has been populated
   119→	// or Delete/Add/Update/AddIfNotPresent was called first.
   120→	populated bool
   121→	// initialPopulationCount is the number of items inserted by the first call of Replace()
   122→	initialPopulationCount int
   123→
   124→	// keyFunc is used to make the key used for queued item
   125→	// insertion and retrieval, and should be deterministic.
   126→	keyFunc KeyFunc
   127→
   128→	// knownObjects list keys that are "known" --- affecting Delete(),
   129→	// Replace(), and Resync()
   130→	knownObjects KeyListerGetter
   131→
   132→	// Used to indicate a queue is closed so a control loop can exit when a queue is empty.
   133→	// Currently, not used to gate any of CRUD operations.
   134→	closed bool
   135→
   136→	// emitDeltaTypeReplaced is whether to emit the Replaced or Sync
   137→	// DeltaType when Replace() is called (to preserve backwards compat).
   138→	emitDeltaTypeReplaced bool
   139→
   140→	// Called with every object if non-nil.
   141→	transformer TransformFunc
   142→
   143→	// logger is a per-instance logger. This gets chosen when constructing
   144→	// the instance, with klog.Background() as default.
   145→	logger klog.Logger
   146→}
   147→

   149→//
   150→// The most common usage pattern is to clean-up some parts of the object to
